text: NVIDIA posted record quarterly revenue of sixty-eight point one billion dollars, up seventy-three percent year over year.
Fortune highlights full-year revenue of two hundred fifteen billion, driven by insatiable AI chip demand.
Bloomberg reports shares tumbled five point five percent after the forecast fell short of expectations.
CNBC notes it was the worst single-day drop in ten months.
Investing.com warns a growing disconnect between record earnings and valuations could signal trouble.
Wall Street now prices AI stocks to perfection, meaning any miss triggers a selloff.
This pattern suggests investors should watch guidance more closely than record revenue numbers.
